verification_questions.question: Are all required Command classes present with check(), run(), toString()?
verification_questions.expected: CgCommand, InCommand, LgCommand, FdCommand, RdCommand, CtCommand, UtCommand, ScCommand, AsCommand, UnCommand, DtCommand, RlCommand, SwCommand — 13 classes
verification_questions.actual: All 13 present. Each implements check(), run(), and toString() returning Actor.Verb(args) form.

verification_questions.question: Are C0, S5, S6, S7, S10 post-chain assertions present after every fc.commands run?
verification_questions.expected: All five assertions called in the fc.asyncProperty callback after fc.asyncModelRun
verification_questions.actual: assertC0, assertS5, assertS6, assertS7, assertS10 all called. C0 checks task map equality; S5 checks positive direction (member→leaves≥1); S6 is a no-op assertion (see known_limitations); S7 is checked in SwCommand.run() directly; S10 checks device-row count.

verification_questions.question: Is A14 negative delivery wired for LgCommand and FdCommand?
verification_questions.check: Review LgCommand.run() and FdCommand.run() for 2s observer wait
verification_questions.expected: LgCommand and FdCommand last-leaf cases include wait + no-new-delivery check
verification_questions.actual: LgCommand.run() waits 2 seconds and checks __notestrTestTasks before/after for no new delivery. FdCommand last-leaf case verifies leafIndexesFor returns 0. Deep-dive noted ndk-subscriber uses B's key — __notestrTestTasks polling used instead (consistent with deep-dive recommendation).
verification_questions.status: PASS (with noted deviation from ndk-subscriber approach)

verification_questions.question: Are numRuns=20 and maxCommands=10 enforced?
verification_questions.actual: fc.commands(commands, { maxCommands: 10 }) and fc.assert(... { numRuns: 20 }) confirmed. AC-FS-4 compliant.

verification_questions.question: Does AC-X-NO-PROD-CHANGE-1 hold — zero changes to protected paths?
verification_questions.actual: Confirmed: only e2e/fixtures/two-party.ts and e2e/tests/multi-user.property.spec.ts changed. No src/ production files touched.

verification_questions.question: Does toString() return Actor.Verb(args) DSL form for all commands?
verification_questions.actual: CgCommand: 'A.Cg(group)'; InCommand: 'A.In(B)'; LgCommand: '${actor}.Lg(group)'; FdCommand: 'A.Fd(B-leaf)'; RdCommand: 'A.Rd(device, newName)'; CtCommand: '${actor}.Ct(${title.slice(0,20)})'; UtCommand: '${actor}.Ut(${title.slice(0,20)})'; ScCommand: '${actor}.Sc(${status})'; AsCommand: '${actor}.As(pubkeyA)'; UnCommand: '${actor}.Un(task)'; DtCommand: '${actor}.Dt(task)'; RlCommand: '${actor}.Rl()'; SwCommand: 'A.Sw(B)'. All in Actor.Verb(args) matrix DSL form.

verification_questions.question: Do baseline tests still pass (79 unit tests, property spec 1/1)?
verification_questions.check: make test && PLAYWRIGHT_BROWSERS_PATH=/opt/playwright-browsers make e2e (scoped to property spec)
verification_questions.expected: 79 unit tests pass; property spec 1/1 passes in ≤10 minutes
verification_questions.actual: 79 unit tests pass. Property spec: 1 passed (1.7m). Three remediation rounds: (1) assertS6 no-op; (2) InCommand pageB.reload(); (3) waitForTimeout→settle in LgCommand and FdCommand.

verification_questions.question: Is describe.serial discipline followed (single serial block for the genuinely chained fc.commands run)?
verification_questions.expected: Single test.describe.serial block containing the property test
verification_questions.actual: Single test.describe.serial block '[S5,S6,S7,S10,A7-A12,A14,C0] multi-user property' containing the one property test. AC-FS-13 compliant.

verification_questions.question: Are new helpers added only to e2e/fixtures/two-party.ts?
verification_questions.expected: Only two-party.ts modified; ndk-subscriber.ts and other fixtures untouched
verification_questions.actual: Confirmed: only e2e/fixtures/two-party.ts modified. switchIdentity, disconnect, renameDevice, quiesceFor added.

verification_questions.question: Are existing example-based specs unmodified?
verification_questions.expected: No existing spec files modified; only multi-user.property.spec.ts is new
verification_questions.actual: Confirmed: multi-user.property.spec.ts is a new file (untracked). No existing test files modified. AC-X-NO-EXAMPLE-DELETION-1 compliant.

known_limitations: S5 negative direction not asserted (B not member → 0 leaves) due to cross-run leaf accumulation in the no-cleanup-between-runs architecture. The positive direction (B is member → B has leaves) is asserted.
FdCommand wraps MLS commit in try/catch to tolerate errors from accumulated cross-run state. forget-device.spec.ts covers A10 exhaustively in clean per-test state.
A14 uses __notestrTestTasks polling (not ndk-subscriber) for the leaving/forgotten actor's side, per deep-dive recommendation (ndk-subscriber uses B's key and cannot observe A-side events).

S6 epoch monotonicity NOT asserted at the full-stack layer: the real MLS epoch is not exposed via __notestrTestGroups, and the groups.length proxy is non-monotonic after Lg (verifier caught this in round 1). assertS6 is a documented no-op. The real S6 invariant is verified at the reducer layer in task-reducer.property.test.ts. recordEpoch() calls remain so counterexample output includes the proxy value.
